prompt: I've been building a personal finance app called Pocketwise for about 6 months now. It started as a budgeting tool but I've been getting feature requests for investment tracking, bill reminders, and shared household budgets. I have no idea what to prioritize next — I'm a solo dev and I can't do everything. My current users are mostly millennials who found me through a Reddit post. Can you help me figure out what to build next and how to think about this strategically?

assertions.text: Correctly identifies this as a Phase 2 (Strategize) entry — does not start full discovery from scratch
Produces at least one persona with behavioral profile (jobs-to-be-done, pain points), not just demographics
Uses an explicit prioritization framework (RICE, Impact-Effort, MoSCoW, or similar) — not just opinion
Priority recommendations cite specific user signals (Reddit users, feature request types, solo dev constraint)
Defines measurable success metrics for the recommended priority
Produces a strategic brief or dispatch recommendation for what to do next
Explicitly states what is NOT being built and why (trade-offs)
